name: Nyatapola Temple
description: Five-story pagoda temple — the tallest in Nepal. Built by King Bhupatindra Malla. Dedicated to Siddhi Lakshmi. Survived both the 1934 and 2015 earthquakes.

name: Krishna Mandir
description: Stone shikhara-style temple dedicated to Krishna. Built by King Siddhi Narsingh Malla. Features 21 golden spires and stone friezes depicting the Mahabharata.

name: Kasthamandap
description: Legendary three-story public rest house said to be made from a single tree. Gave Kathmandu its name. Collapsed in the 2015 earthquake; reconstruction ongoing.

name: Dharahara
description: Nine-story watchtower built by Prime Minister Bhimsen Thapa. Collapsed in 2015 earthquake. Rebuilt in 2021 with earthquake-resistant design.

name: 55-Window Palace
description: Royal palace known for its balcony with 55 intricately carved wooden windows. Built during the reign of King Yaksha Malla. Houses the National Art Gallery.

name: Golden Gate (Sun Dhoka)
description: Gilt copper gate considered the finest example of metalwork in the Valley. Entrance to the Taleju Chowk of the Royal Palace. Features Garuda, nagas, and ten-armed Taleju.

name: Bagh Bhairav Temple
description: Temple dedicated to the tiger form of Bhairav. Walls covered with weapons from the Gorkha conquest. Important religious site for Kirtipur's Newar community.

name: Rani Pokhari
description: Artificial pond built by King Pratap Malla in memory of his son. Features a Shiva temple in the center. Restored after 2015 earthquake damage.

name: Patan Museum
description: Former Malla royal residence converted to museum in 1997. Houses one of South Asia's finest collections of bronze statues and religious art. Austrian-restored.

name: Mayadevi Temple
description: Temple at the exact birthplace of Buddha. Contains the marker stone and sacred pool (Puskarni). Excavations revealed pre-Ashokan brick structures dating to 6th century BCE.
